# PyFixest

> Fast fixest-style high-dimensional fixed-effects estimation, inference, and reporting in Python.

## Start here

- [PyFixest](https://pyfixest.org/pyfixest.html.md): Fast fixest-style high-dimensional fixed-effects estimation, inference, and reporting in Python.
- [A first Regression with PyFixest](https://pyfixest.org/getting-started.html.md): Install PyFixest and fit, inspect, and plot a first fixed-effects regression.
- [Getting Started with PyFixest](https://pyfixest.org/quickstart.html.md): A compact introduction to OLS, fixed effects, standard errors, and result access in PyFixest.
- [Troubleshooting](https://pyfixest.org/troubleshooting.html.md): Recover from common formula, inference, stored-data, optional-dependency, and reporting errors in PyFixest.

## Tutorials

- [Tutorials](https://pyfixest.org/tutorials/index.html.md): End-to-end PyFixest tutorials for formulas, estimators, inference, and regression reporting.
- [OLS with Fixed Effects](https://pyfixest.org/tutorials/ols-fixed-effects.html.md): Twin studies and 'AKM' regressions in worker-firm panels: two cases studies in controlling for unobserved heterogeneity with fixed effects.
- [Formula Syntax](https://pyfixest.org/tutorials/formula-syntax.html.md): In this tutorial, we showcase all of PyFixest formulas syntax; including syntax for fitting models with fixed effects, interactions, and multiple-estimation operators.
- [Instrumental Variables](https://pyfixest.org/tutorials/instrumental-variables.html.md): IV estimation with fixed effects, first-stage diagnostics, and the effective F-statistic.
- [Poisson & GLMs](https://pyfixest.org/tutorials/poisson-glm.html.md): Poisson for nonnegative outcomes, DiD with counts, and logit/probit for propensity-score based AB testing.
- [Quantile Regression](https://pyfixest.org/tutorials/quantile-regression.html.md): Quantile regression for modeling the full conditional distribution, not just the mean.
- [Regression Tables](https://pyfixest.org/tutorials/regression-tables.html.md): Publication-ready tables with Great Tables or LaTeX booktabs — customize labels, significance stars, and output formats.
- [Standard Errors & Inference](https://pyfixest.org/tutorials/standard-errors.html.md): HC-robust, cluster-robust, wild bootstrap, randomization inference, and the causal cluster variance estimator.
- [Difference-in-Differences Estimation](https://pyfixest.org/tutorials/difference-in-differences.html.md): TWFE, Gardner's two-stage DID2S, local projections, and event study designs with heterogeneous treatment effects.

## How-to guides

- [Guides](https://pyfixest.org/how-to/index.html.md): Practical PyFixest recipes for inference, decomposition, demeaners, interoperability, and experimental workflows.
- [Choosing a Demeaner Backend](https://pyfixest.org/how-to/demeaner-backends.html.md): How to pick the right fixed-effects demeaner for your problem.
- [Marginal Effects and Hypothesis Tests via `marginaleffects`](https://pyfixest.org/how-to/marginaleffects.html.md): Compute marginal effects, predictions, and hypothesis tests from PyFixest models with marginaleffects.
- [Variance Reduction in AB Tests](https://pyfixest.org/how-to/panel_variance_reduction.html.md): How to reduce the variance of estimators in AB tests and beyond using exogenous control variables via CUPED, linear regression, and fixed effects estimators.
- [How much do Covariates Matter?](https://pyfixest.org/how-to/regression_decomposition.html.md): Decompose coefficient changes into covariate contributions with the Gelbach decomposition.
- [Translating Stata to PyFixest](https://pyfixest.org/how-to/stata-2-pyfixest.html.md): Translate common Stata regression, fixed-effect, inference, and reporting workflows to PyFixest.

## Explanation

- [When Are Fixed Effects Estimations Difficult?](https://pyfixest.org/explanation/difficult-fixed-effects.html.md): Why fixed-effect graph structure controls convergence and how PyFixest demeaners perform on difficult designs.
- [On Small Sample Corrections](https://pyfixest.org/explanation/ssc.html.md): How PyFixest applies fixest-compatible small-sample corrections for robust and clustered inference.
- [Does `PyFixest` match `fixest`?](https://pyfixest.org/explanation/compare-fixest-pyfixest.html.md): Numerical comparisons between PyFixest and R fixest across estimation and inference features.
- [Case Studies and Blog Posts](https://pyfixest.org/explanation/blog-posts.html.md): External case studies, tutorials, and practical examples that use PyFixest.

## API reference

- [PyFixest function reference](https://pyfixest.org/reference/index.html.md): Index of public estimators, result classes, reporting functions, formula helpers, and utilities.
- [feols](https://pyfixest.org/reference/estimation.api.feols.feols.html.md): OLS, WLS, and instrumental-variables estimation with high-dimensional fixed effects.
- [fepois](https://pyfixest.org/reference/estimation.api.fepois.fepois.html.md): Poisson regression with high-dimensional fixed effects.
- [feglm](https://pyfixest.org/reference/estimation.api.feglm.feglm.html.md): Generalized linear models with high-dimensional fixed effects.
- [quantreg](https://pyfixest.org/reference/estimation.api.quantreg.quantreg.html.md): Quantile regression for one or more conditional quantiles.
- [did2s](https://pyfixest.org/reference/did.estimation.did2s.html.md): Gardner two-stage difference-in-differences estimation.
- [lpdid](https://pyfixest.org/reference/did.estimation.lpdid.html.md): Local-projections difference-in-differences estimation.
- [event_study](https://pyfixest.org/reference/did.estimation.event_study.html.md): Event-study estimation through supported difference-in-differences estimators.
- [SaturatedEventStudy](https://pyfixest.org/reference/did.saturated_twfe.SaturatedEventStudy.html.md): Saturated cohort-specific event-study estimator.
- [panelview](https://pyfixest.org/reference/did.visualize.panelview.html.md): Visualize panel treatment timing and outcomes.
- [bonferroni](https://pyfixest.org/reference/estimation.post_estimation.multcomp.bonferroni.html.md): Bonferroni-adjusted p-values across fitted models.
- [rwolf](https://pyfixest.org/reference/estimation.post_estimation.multcomp.rwolf.html.md): Romano-Wolf stepdown-adjusted p-values across fitted models.
- [wyoung](https://pyfixest.org/reference/estimation.post_estimation.multcomp.wyoung.html.md): Westfall-Young adjusted p-values across fitted models.
- [BaseDemeaner](https://pyfixest.org/reference/demeaners.BaseDemeaner.html.md): Protocol implemented by fixed-effects demeaning backends.
- [MapDemeaner](https://pyfixest.org/reference/demeaners.MapDemeaner.html.md): Alternating-projections fixed-effects demeaner.
- [LsmrDemeaner](https://pyfixest.org/reference/demeaners.LsmrDemeaner.html.md): Sparse LSMR fixed-effects demeaner.
- [Preconditioner](https://pyfixest.org/reference/core.demean.Preconditioner.html.md): Reusable preconditioner for compatible within-LSMR fixed-effects designs.
- [Feols](https://pyfixest.org/reference/estimation.models.feols_.Feols.html.md): Fitted OLS and WLS result contract and post-estimation methods.
- [Feiv](https://pyfixest.org/reference/estimation.models.feiv_.Feiv.html.md): Fitted instrumental-variables result contract and diagnostic methods.
- [Fepois](https://pyfixest.org/reference/estimation.models.fepois_.Fepois.html.md): Fitted Poisson fixed-effects result contract.
- [Feglm](https://pyfixest.org/reference/estimation.models.feglm_.Feglm.html.md): Base fitted generalized-linear-model result contract.
- [Felogit](https://pyfixest.org/reference/estimation.models.felogit_.Felogit.html.md): Fitted fixed-effects logit result contract.
- [Feprobit](https://pyfixest.org/reference/estimation.models.feprobit_.Feprobit.html.md): Fitted fixed-effects probit result contract.
- [Fegaussian](https://pyfixest.org/reference/estimation.models.fegaussian_.Fegaussian.html.md): Fitted Gaussian GLM result contract.
- [FixestMulti](https://pyfixest.org/reference/estimation.FixestMulti_.FixestMulti.html.md): Container and post-estimation interface for multiple fitted models.
- [Quantreg](https://pyfixest.org/reference/estimation.quantreg.quantreg_.Quantreg.html.md): Fitted quantile-regression result contract.
- [summary](https://pyfixest.org/reference/report.summary.html.md): Print summaries for one or more fitted models.
- [etable](https://pyfixest.org/reference/report.etable.html.md): Build regression tables from fitted models.
- [dtable](https://pyfixest.org/reference/report.dtable.html.md): Build descriptive-statistics tables.
- [coefplot](https://pyfixest.org/reference/report.coefplot.html.md): Plot coefficients and confidence intervals.
- [iplot](https://pyfixest.org/reference/report.iplot.html.md): Plot coefficients created by formula interactions.
- [qplot](https://pyfixest.org/reference/report.qplot.html.md): Plot coefficients over quantile-regression results.
- [Formula](https://pyfixest.org/reference/estimation.formula.parse.Formula.html.md): Parsed fixest-style formula representation.
- [ModelMatrix](https://pyfixest.org/reference/estimation.formula.model_matrix.ModelMatrix.html.md): Model-matrix representation used by the estimation planner.
- [factor_interaction](https://pyfixest.org/reference/estimation.formula.factor_interaction.factor_interaction.html.md): Build factor interactions for the fixest-style i() formula operator.
- [demean](https://pyfixest.org/reference/estimation.demean.html.md): Demean arrays by one or more high-dimensional fixed effects.
- [model_matrix_fixest](https://pyfixest.org/reference/estimation.deprecated.model_matrix_fixest_.model_matrix_fixest.html.md): Deprecated compatibility entry point for constructing fixest-style model matrices.
- [detect_singletons](https://pyfixest.org/reference/core.detect_singletons.detect_singletons.html.md): Detect singleton observations in fixed-effect groups.
- [ssc](https://pyfixest.org/reference/utils.utils.ssc.html.md): Configure small-sample corrections for inference.
- [get_ssc](https://pyfixest.org/reference/utils.utils.get_ssc.html.md): Inspect the active small-sample-correction configuration.
- [get_data](https://pyfixest.org/reference/utils.utils.get_data.html.md): Load PyFixest's packaged example data.
- [get_bartik_data](https://pyfixest.org/reference/utils.dgps.get_bartik_data.html.md): Generate a packaged shift-share instrumental-variables example.
- [get_encouragement_data](https://pyfixest.org/reference/utils.dgps.get_encouragement_data.html.md): Generate a randomized encouragement-design example.
- [get_ivf_data](https://pyfixest.org/reference/utils.dgps.get_ivf_data.html.md): Generate a synthetic IVF instrumental-variables example.
- [get_motherhood_event_study_data](https://pyfixest.org/reference/utils.dgps.get_motherhood_event_study_data.html.md): Generate a motherhood event-study example.
- [get_twin_data](https://pyfixest.org/reference/utils.dgps.get_twin_data.html.md): Generate a synthetic twin-based instrumental-variables example.
- [get_worker_panel](https://pyfixest.org/reference/utils.dgps.get_worker_panel.html.md): Generate a worker-panel fixed-effects example.

## Optional

- [PyFixest architecture](https://pyfixest.org/architecture.html.md): Canonical guide to dependency direction, estimation flow, model capabilities, state, caches, Rust kernels, and numerical tests.
- [Contributing](https://pyfixest.org/contributing.html.md): Set up a PyFixest development environment, find architectural guidance, and submit tested, documented changes.
- [Changelog](https://pyfixest.org/changelog.html.md): Release-by-release changes to PyFixest APIs, estimators, inference, performance, and documentation.
- [Acknowledgements](https://pyfixest.org/acknowledgements.html.md): Packages, papers, maintainers, and funders whose work shaped PyFixest.
- [Surrogate Indices & Experimental Selection](https://pyfixest.org/how-to/surrogate-indices-experimental-selection.html.md): Outline for estimating long-run effects with surrogates and applying experimental selection correction in PyFixest.
- [Textbook Replications](https://pyfixest.org/textbook-replications/index.html.md): PyFixest translations of examples from widely used causal-inference textbooks.
- [Causal Inference for the Brave and True](https://pyfixest.org/textbook-replications/brave_true.html.md): PyFixest replications of panel-data and fixed-effects examples from Causal Inference for the Brave and True.
- [The Mixtape with PyFixest](https://pyfixest.org/textbook-replications/mixtape.html.md): PyFixest translations of selected examples from Causal Inference: The Mixtape.
- [Replicating Examples from "The Effect"](https://pyfixest.org/textbook-replications/replicating-the-effect.html.md): PyFixest replications of causal-inference examples from The Effect.
- [Learning Econometrics with PyFixest](https://pyfixest.org/textbook-replications/resources.html.md): Books, courses, and teaching resources for learning econometrics with PyFixest.
